evals.prompt: Refine my Fluid theme so the homepage matches the source site. Source is https://yellowbirdfoods.com, my Fluid store is https://companyname.fluid.app, theme ID 55697, token PT-xxx. Theme is checked out at /Users/me/themes/yellowbird.
evals.expected_output: Should validate inputs (source reachable, Fluid API works, theme ID resolves) AND run the local toolchain preflight (Node ≥ 18, fluid CLI + theme-dev plugin, fluid whoami, Playwright + Chromium). Should confirm company identity before any changes. Should pull the theme, run `fluid theme dev` for localhost preview, run Playwright visual diff at desktop/tablet/mobile, classify findings as auto-fix vs flag, apply auto-fixes by saving section files (watcher uploads), loop until findings = 0 or remaining are flagged.
assertions: Validates source URL reachable
Validates Fluid API token and theme ID
Runs the local toolchain preflight: node, fluid CLI, fluid theme --help, fluid whoami, Playwright + Chromium
If any preflight tool is missing, surfaces the exact install command and asks before installing
Never installs Playwright or other tooling silently
Confirms company identity before proceeding
Does not proceed without explicit user confirmation of the right store + theme
Pulls the theme to a local directory before refining
Runs `fluid theme dev --port 9292` from the theme directory
Waits for the dev server to be ready before running the diff
Runs Playwright comparing source vs http://127.0.0.1:9292/
Captures paired screenshots at desktop (1440x900), tablet (768x1024), mobile (390x844)
Walks each captured page section-by-section
Reads source/built PNG pairs and classifies findings as auto-fix vs flag
Applies auto-fixes by editing section files locally — does NOT push via API during the loop
Relies on the `fluid theme dev` watcher to upload changes and hot-reload
Loops until findings converge or remaining are all flagged
Surfaces flagged findings to the user before declaring complete

evals.prompt: I want to modernize this old legacy Fluid theme to the gold standard. It has Splide everywhere, every section uses raw hex colors, no Section Shell pattern. Theme is at /Users/me/themes/legacy. Token PT-xxx, Fluid URL https://test.fluid.app, theme ID 12345.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is workflow B (legacy modernization), not workflow A (pixel-perfect). Should run Phase 0 architecture audit FIRST (theme-wide files + grep audits) before any visual work. Should use the rubric to decide refine vs re-clone, apply Recipe Book transformations, then run dev-preview visual verification only after the architectural fixes land.
assertions: Identifies this as legacy modernization (workflow B)
Runs Phase 0 audit before any visual diff work
Audits theme-wide files: settings_schema.json, settings_data.json, config.css, reset.css, theme.liquid, pagination component
Runs the theme-wide greps for Splide, render targeting blocks/, font_picker in sections, raw hex defaults, body overflow:hidden, image_picker on content images, navbar JS hooks
Tables the findings with file:line:issue
Applies the Phase 0d migration table to map legacy sections to v4 base-theme equivalents
Uses the Recipe Book for in-section transformations
Fixes architectural findings BEFORE visual diff
Then runs `fluid theme dev` and Playwright visual verification at desktop/tablet/mobile

evals.prompt: The hero section on my homepage doesn't match the source. Just fix that one section. Source https://example.com, theme at /Users/me/themes/example, theme ID 99 on https://demo.fluid.app, token PT-xxx.
evals.expected_output: Should skip the full-page sweep and target just the hero. Should run the toolchain preflight, start `fluid theme dev`, run Playwright comparison scoped to the hero section pair (sec01) at all three breakpoints. Auto-fix mechanical mismatches; flag judgement calls.
assertions: Confirms inputs and runs toolchain preflight
Spins up `fluid theme dev`
Runs Playwright diff but focuses on the hero section pair (sec01)
Captures hero at all three breakpoints (desktop, tablet, mobile)
Classifies findings as auto-fix vs flag
Applies auto-fixes to sections/.../hero/index.liquid only
Does not modify other sections
Loops on the hero until converged or flagged-only

evals.prompt: I'm in the middle of a refinement and I keep doing `curl -X PUT /api/application_themes/.../resources` to push my fixed sections. The page never updates. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that during a refine loop, the agent should be running `fluid theme dev` which has a filesystem watcher that auto-uploads on save. Manual API pushes during the loop are unnecessary and cause the watcher to fight with cache. Should tell the user to start the dev server and edit files locally — the watcher handles the rest.
assertions: Identifies that the user is missing `fluid theme dev`
Tells the user to start `fluid theme dev` in their theme directory
Explains that the watcher auto-uploads changed files on save
Explains that the localhost preview hot-reloads automatically
Notes that manual API pushes during the loop are not needed
Lists the rare cases where a manual push IS still needed (binaries, template-destroy preset refresh)

evals.prompt: Refinement found these diffs on the homepage hero: 1) bg-color #1B3A4B vs #1C3B4C, 2) heading font-size 56px vs 48px, 3) source uses a 3-column features grid, mine has 2 columns, 4) source has a video, mine has a static image. What do you fix?
evals.expected_output: Should classify each finding: bg-color = auto-fix (numeric, theme token), font-size = auto-fix (numeric), 3-col vs 2-col layout = FLAG (structural change), video vs image = FLAG (asset swap + media type change). Should auto-fix #1 and #2 directly, surface #3 and #4 to the user before changing.
assertions: Classifies bg-color mismatch as auto-fix
Classifies font-size mismatch as auto-fix
Classifies 2-col vs 3-col layout as FLAG (do not auto-fix)
Classifies video vs static image as FLAG (do not auto-fix)
Applies bg-color and font-size fixes directly to section files
Does NOT change the column count without user confirmation
Does NOT swap image for video without user confirmation
Surfaces the two flagged findings to the user with explanation
Asks the user for direction before continuing

evals.prompt: Make my theme match the source at desktop, tablet, AND mobile. Theme at /Users/me/themes/site, theme ID 42, token PT-xxx, Fluid URL https://my.fluid.app, source https://source.com.
evals.expected_output: Should run a single `fluid theme dev` instance and a single Playwright diff that captures all three breakpoints (1440x900, 768x1024, 390x844) in one run. Should walk desktop pairs first, then tablet, then mobile — applying fixes between rounds. Should not require three separate runs.
assertions: Spins up `fluid theme dev` once for the entire run
Runs Playwright once per round, capturing all three breakpoints in one invocation
Walks desktop pairs first (catches structural / typographic issues)
Then walks tablet pairs
Then walks mobile pairs (often surfaces hamburger / padding-collapse issues)
Applies auto-fixes between rounds, not at the end
Each round uses the same `--label` so output overwrites cleanly
Does not run separate scripts per breakpoint

evals.prompt: I'm refining my theme but `fluid theme dev` says 'unknown command'. What now?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose this as a missing theme-dev plugin. Should surface the install command (`npm install -g @fluid-app/fluid-cli-theme-dev`) and ask the user before installing. Should also mention `enabledPlugins` in `~/.fluid/config.json` as a possible cause if installed. Should NOT silently install.
assertions: Diagnoses 'unknown command' as missing theme-dev plugin
Surfaces the install command: npm install -g @fluid-app/fluid-cli-theme-dev
Asks the user before running the install
Mentions enabledPlugins in ~/.fluid/config.json as another possible cause
Does not silently run npm install

evals.prompt: The Phase 0 audit on my legacy theme found 35 findings: every section has hex defaults, Splide is everywhere, no Section Shell pattern, no canonical blocks. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Should apply the Phase 0c rubric. With 35 findings spanning structural patterns + visual + asset, this is a re-clone candidate, not refine. Should recommend running `/fluid-theme-clone` against the existing site instead. Should explain why (faster than rebuilding section by section).
assertions: Applies the Phase 0c refine-vs-reclone rubric
Identifies 35 findings + structural breadth as a re-clone candidate
Recommends running `/fluid-theme-clone` instead
Explains the reasoning: faster than rebuilding section by section
Does not start refining when the rubric points to re-clone

evals.prompt: After refining, the hero matches source but the section below it now has weird padding. Did I break something?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that fixes can have side effects on adjacent sections (CSS specificity, container assumptions). Should re-run the Playwright diff and read the section pair below the hero to confirm. Should not silently move on without verifying.
assertions: Acknowledges fixes can affect adjacent sections
Re-runs the Playwright diff after a fix lands
Reads section pairs adjacent to the just-edited section, not just the target
If the side-effect is real, treats it as a new finding (auto-fix vs flag)
Does not declare the page complete based only on the originally-fixed section

evals.prompt: After 5 rounds of fixes, the testimonial section still looks slightly off — quote font is some custom serif we don't have. The diff keeps flagging it.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this hits the 'after 5 rounds without convergence' rule. Should freeze the section, document the remaining delta in a code comment in the section's Liquid file, log it in the final report, and move on. Should not loop indefinitely.
assertions: Recognizes the 5-round convergence threshold
Identifies custom font as a non-fixable / FLAG finding
Documents the remaining delta in a code comment in the section file
Logs the unresolved finding in the final report
Stops looping on this section
Suggests the closest available font as a substitution
Does not silently abandon the issue

evals.prompt: Refine my theme but I need to do this offline — I'm on a flight. Theme is at /Users/me/themes/x. Source is https://example.com, token PT-xxx, theme ID 100, Fluid URL https://test.fluid.app.
evals.expected_output: Should explain that `fluid theme dev` requires authentication and reaches the live storefront — it doesn't work offline. Should refuse to start the visual-diff loop. Should offer to do structural/Phase 0 audits offline (file-based grep + lint) but defer visual work until network access is restored.
assertions: Explains `fluid theme dev` is not a static local renderer
Notes that the dev server proxies the live storefront and requires network + auth
Refuses to start the visual-diff loop offline
Offers to do Phase 0 architectural audit offline (purely file-based)
Defers visual diff work until network access is restored
